jobs.title: Executive Business Partner to CEO
jobs.description:
The Big Picture

Our client is an innovative technology company developing next-generation energy solutions that are changing the way connected devices are powered. With a collaborative culture, ambitious growth plans, and a leadership team focused on innovation, they are looking for an exceptional Executive Business Partner to become a trusted extension of their executive team.

As Executive Business Partner, you’ll be the operational backbone and strategic partner to a dynamic CEO while also supporting a small group of senior leaders. You’ll sit at the center of the business, helping executives stay focused on what matters most, coordinating board activities, creating exceptional experiences for customers and visitors, and helping build a workplace where people genuinely enjoy showing up every day.
If you’re the type of person who naturally sees what needs to happen before anyone asks, thrives in a fast-paced environment, and loves being the person who keeps everything running smoothly, we’d love to meet you.

What You’ll Tackle

Serve as the primary partner to the CEO and support additional members of the executive leadership team.

Manage complex calendars, scheduling, travel, and expenses across multiple executives.

Coordinate Board of Directors meetings, materials, communications, and logistics.

Support the CEO’s external board commitments and select personal scheduling responsibilities.

Help keep executive priorities organized and moving forward.

Welcome and interact with customers, investors, board members, and other key stakeholders.

Oversee office operations and help create a productive, engaging workplace environment.

Plan team events, celebrations, and culture-building activities.

Jump into special projects and solve problems before they become problems.

What You Bring

Significant experience supporting C-suite executives in a fast-paced environment.

Experience managing complex calendars, travel, and executive priorities.

Prior board administration experience.

A track record of handling confidential information with discretion and professionalism.

Experience in a startup, high-growth, or technology-driven company is highly preferred.

Experience supporting founders or CEOs.

Office management experience.

Employee engagement, culture, or event planning experience.

Experience serving as a true business partner rather than simply an administrative resource.

You Tend to Be . . .

Three steps ahead.

Calm under pressure.

Incredibly organized without being rigid.

Resourceful, proactive, and highly dependable.

The person people trust to get things done.

Positive, approachable, and energizing to be around.

Someone who genuinely enjoys making life easier for others.

The Perks & Practicalities

Location: San Jose, CA
Compensation: $130k-$150k
Benefits: Comprehensive benefits package
Work Model  : Hybrid

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Mid-Senior level

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Administrative

Industries

Appliances, Electrical, and Electronics Manufacturing

jobs.company: HATCHER GARDEN and WOODLAND PRESERVE INC
jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
Executive Director (Full-Time, Salaried)

Hatcher Garden and Woodland Preserve is a 13-acre privately owned botanical garden open to the public for free. Located in the heart of Spartanburg, SC our garden is dedicated to horticultural education, conservation, and community engagement. A beloved community institution, Hatcher provides a welcoming green space for visitors of all ages while serving as a hub for environmental stewardship and civic partnership.

Position Summary

The Executive Director is the senior leader of the organization, responsible for overall organizational leadership, strategic plan adherence, fundraising, financial oversight, public relations, donor stewardship, supervision of staff, and cultivation of relationships with community volunteers. The Executive Director works closely with the Board of Directors to advance the mission, visibility, and long-term sustainability of the organization.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

Qualifications
•     Bachelor’s degree preferred in nonprofit management, business administration, communications, horticulture, public administration, or related field.
•     Demonstrated experience in nonprofit leadership and fundraising.
•     Demonstrated ability to build and sustain relationships with individual donors, foundations, and community partners.
•     Strong written and verbal communication skills.
•     Experience with grant writing and donor relations.
•     Competency with QuickBooks and/or Raiser’s Edge software.
•     Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.
•     Ability to work on occasional evenings and weekends for events and donor activities.

Fundraising and Development
•     Cultivate and solicit individual, corporate, and foundation donors.
•     Build and sustain relationships with donors, sponsors, and community partners through regular, meaningful engagement.
•     Prepare and present grant applications, sponsorship proposals, and fundraising presentations.
•     Assist with planning and execution of fundraising events and campaigns.

Organizational Leadership
•     Provide leadership and oversight for all garden operations, programs, and administrative functions.
•     Implement policies and strategic initiatives established by the Board of Directors.
•     Supervise and evaluate staff members and coordinate departmental operations.
•     Foster a positive organizational culture among staff, volunteers, donors, and community partners.

Financial Management
•     Oversee organizational budgeting and financial reporting.
•     Maintain proficiency in QuickBooks and/or Raiser’s Edge software.
•     Review deposits, expenditures, and financial procedures for accuracy and accountability.
•     Work collaboratively with accountants, auditors, and the Board Treasurer.

Administrative and Technology Responsibilities
•     Maintain advanced proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, including Word, Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint.
•     Prepare reports, correspondence, presentations, and organizational documents.
•     Oversee donor databases and records management systems.

Public Relations and Visitor Engagement
•     Serve as a public ambassador for the Garden at meetings, civic events, and community functions.
•     Lead tours for donors, sponsors, and special guests with knowledge of garden history, horticultural collections, structures, and special features.
•     Support marketing, communications, and public outreach efforts.

Board Relations
•     Attend Board and committee meetings as requested.
•     Prepare reports and recommendations for Board review.
•     Assist with strategic planning and policy development.

Physical Requirements
•     Ability to walk garden grounds and conduct outdoor tours.
•     Ability to attend outdoor events in varying weather conditions.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Executive

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Business Development and Sales

Industries

Zoos and Botanical Gardens

jobs.title: Chief Program Officer, The AI Access Initiative
jobs.description:
1. About Our Client:
The AI Access Initiative (2AI) operates within the artificial intelligence sector, focusing on leveraging AI innovations to address poverty in low- and middle-income countries. The organization aims to bridge the development gap by scaling AI-enabled programs that benefit tens or hundreds of millions of people living in poverty. It collaborates with global development actors, AI labs, and researchers to create open-sourced resources and implement impactful AI solutions in agriculture and health, with outreach already impacting millions in countries such as India.

2. About the Opportunity:
The Chief Program Officer will lead and scale programmatic efforts across agriculture and health portfolios, playing a critical role in expanding AI-enabled interventions to reach tens of millions of beneficiaries. This position involves setting program strategy, building and managing teams, and establishing systems to ensure effective program delivery and learning. As a key leadership team member, the Chief Program Officer influences the organization’s direction and represents it to partners and funders.

3. Responsibilities:
• Serve on the executive leadership team, shaping strategy, culture, and priorities.
• Hire, mentor, and develop leadership and program staff.
• Establish rigorous learning architectures with clear hypotheses, measurement, iteration, and reporting.
• Guide resource allocation based on cost-effectiveness evidence.
• Develop and maintain open-sourced playbooks and insights.
• Set and execute ambitious program strategies and expansion plans.
• Collaborate closely with regional and program leaders.
• Build program teams, systems, and partnerships to support scaling.
• Engage with funders and external stakeholders as a key organizational representative.

4. Requirements:
• Experience leading large-scale program operations, preferably across multiple geographies.
• Strong ability to recruit and develop high-performing teams.
• Expertise in designing program strategy and organizational infrastructure.
• Skills in implementing learning and measurement processes.
• Ability to work collaboratively with diverse stakeholders including governments and partners.
• Experience in the global development, AI, health, or agriculture sectors is advantageous.

5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:
• The pay range and compensation package for this role will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, skills, and other relevant factors.

Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.

Note:
RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Executive

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Strategy/Planning

Industries

Software Development

jobs.title: Associate Director of Strategic Partnerships
jobs.description:
1. About Our Client:
The organization supports transformative Fellowships designed in partnership with leading corporations and foundations to aid entrepreneurs and leaders from underserved backgrounds. These Fellowships provide entrepreneurship and leadership training, mentorship, community stipends, long-term alumni support, and access to a global network of peers and opportunities. With partnerships involving major entities such as Google, JPMorganChase Foundation, Walmart.org, Wells Fargo, and others, the program reaches over 2,400 emerging leaders annually. Alumni have collectively raised over $190 million, created more than 2,300 paid jobs, and impacted over 11 million people globally, with a high rate of continued employment, education, or venture creation.

2. About the Opportunity:
The Associate Director of Strategic Partnerships is responsible for developing and expanding a portfolio of corporate and foundation partnerships that support the organization''s Fellowships. This role focuses on creating and maintaining strategic collaborations that align business and community impact goals, ensuring the growth and sustainability of the Fellowship programs.

3. Responsibilities:
• Build and grow partnerships with corporations and foundations to support Fellowship programs.
• Create tailored and persuasive impact updates including decks, emails, and profiles for prospects and partners.
• Lead and prepare for prospect and partner meetings, including managing follow-ups and coordinating with internal teams.
• Map and engage complex stakeholder networks within partner organizations.
• Develop concept notes, decks, and grant applications demonstrating both business and community impact.
• Design points of connection that align partnerships with corporate strategy and goals across multiple teams.
• Coordinate closely with senior leadership to achieve partnership objectives.
• Collaborate with the Design Team on monitoring, evaluation, and learning to meet partner expectations.
• Lead special projects critical to partnership strategy, such as market landscape analyses and thought leadership development.

4. Requirements:
• Strong attention to detail with excellent organizational and project management skills.
• Proven ability to create compelling, well-designed communications and presentations.
• Experience managing complex stakeholder engagement.
• Strategic thinking with the ability to design and implement effective systems and processes.
• High integrity and sound judgment in handling sensitive information and decision-making.
• Results-driven with a proactive and persistent approach to responsibilities.

5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:
• The pay range and compensation package for this role will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, skills, and other relevant factors.

Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.

Note:
RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

jobs.title: Executive Assistant to Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
Company Description
Buena Vista Kennels is a consumer services company located in Hollister, California, serving clients at its Buena Vista Road location and beyond. The organization focuses on providing reliable, high-quality care and services for pets, with an emphasis on client trust and satisfaction. Team members collaborate closely to deliver safe, attentive, and professional pet care experiences. As the company grows, it is strengthening its operational and executive support functions to ensure smooth service delivery and sustainable expansion. Working with Buena Vista Kennels offers the opportunity to contribute to a mission-driven environment centered on pet wellbeing and client service.
Role Description
The Executive Assistant to the Chief Executive Officer is a full-time remote role responsible for providing high-level administrative and operational support to the CEO. Day-to-day tasks include managing the CEO’s calendar, scheduling meetings, coordinating virtual and in-person appointments, and organizing travel arrangements. The role also involves preparing and tracking expense reports, handling confidential correspondence, and maintaining accurate records and documentation. The Executive Assistant will help streamline communication, prioritize incoming requests, and support follow-up on key initiatives and deadlines. This position requires proactive coordination with internal and external stakeholders, preparation of meeting materials, and support for special projects that advance the company’s strategic and operational goals.
Qualifications

Strong Executive Administrative Assistance and Executive Support skills, with experience supporting senior leadership.
Proficiency in Diary Management, including complex calendar coordination, scheduling, and meeting logistics.
Ability to prepare, review, and manage Expense Reports with accuracy and attention to detail.
Solid Administrative Assistance capabilities, including document preparation, data entry, and record management.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills and professional email and document etiquette.
High level of discretion and ability to handle confidential information responsibly.
Comfort working remotely, with strong time management, organizational skills, and self-direction.
Proficiency with productivity and collaboration tools (e.g., Microsoft Office or Google Workspace, virtual meeting platforms).
Previous experience in an executive assistant or similar role; experience in a services or pet care environment is a plus.
Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Communications, or a related field preferred, or equivalent experience.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Mid-Senior level

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Administrative

Industries

Consumer Services

jobs.title: Director of Development for the College of Applied Science and Technology
jobs.description:
This job was posted by https://illinoisjoblink.illinois.gov : For more

Information, Please See

https://illinoisjoblink.illinois.gov/jobs/13586440

The Director of Development serves as the development liaison to the

College of Applied Science and Technology (the College) and represents

the College in pursuing major gifts (funding commitments of \$25,000 or

more made via outright, pledged or planned gift arrangements). The

individual holding this position will develop and execute strategies and

plans to cultivate, solicit and secure major gifts and qualify new major

gift prospects. The individual will work in close collaboration with the

Dean of the College and will work as needed with other administrators,

faculty, and staff of the College. As appropriate, the individual will

collaborate with other development officers working on behalf of the

Illinois State University\'s other colleges and administrative units.\

\

Primary Responsibilities Will Be Implemented Through Regular And

frequent visits and other substantive contacts with constituents to

facilitate major gift support. Significant travel will be an essential

part of the position, as well as occasional participation in evening and

weekend activities.
